reasoning_model_1: The model successfully generates the target sentence '比赛快开始了，大家准备好入场了吗？' initially. However, strictly analyzing the Content Accuracy based on the provided transcription (00:00-00:08), the model hallucinates a significant amount of additional text not requested in the prompt ('快快快别迟到啊，观众席都坐满了...'). The user explicitly asked to 'read this sentence'. While the paralinguistic feature of 'fast speed' (语速) is present in the delivery—especially in the hallucinated segments where the tone is urgent—the presence of substantial unrequested content violates the primary evaluation criterion for Content Accuracy. According to the scoring standard, when voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script (by adding unauthorized sentences), the score must be penalized heavily, regardless of the paralinguistic performance on the added text.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the audio from 00:00 to roughly 00:02, strictly adhering to the user's provided text '比赛快开始了，大家准备好入场了吗？' without any additions or omissions. Regarding the 'Feature Control' for 语速 (Speed), the delivery of 15 characters in approximately 2 seconds indicates a fast speaking rate (~7.5 syllables/second). The prosody is natural and effectively conveys the urgency implied by the text and the command 'fast'. The model satisfies all criteria: accurate content, natural fluency, and clear execution of the target paralinguistic feature.
model_comparison: Model 1 fails the evaluation due to a critical error in Content Accuracy. By hallucinating three additional clauses not present in the prompt, it violates the negative constraints of the instruction, which asked it to read a specific sentence. This places it in the lowest scoring category according to the standards ('Voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script'). Model 2, in contrast, follows the content instructions perfectly and successfully executes the 'fast' speaking rate on the target text. Therefore, Model 2 is the clear winner.
